chunks.title: Catalog Description
chunks.content: Economic Analysis of Engineering Projects. (2-0). Credit 2. Principles of economic equivalence; time value of money; analysis of single and multiple investments; comparison of alternatives; capital recovery and after-tax analysis of economic projects. Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in MATH 152 or MATH 172; also taught at Qatar campus.

chunks.title: Special Course Designation
chunks.content: None

chunks.title: Regarding Course Materials
chunks.content: Materials such as but not limited to lecture notes, quizzes, homework, practice exams, exams, etc. that are provided as part of this course are intended solely for your personal academic use and it is not allowed to distribute these materials by any means or upload them to any other website unless you have been granted written permission to do so.

chunks.title: Grading Policy
chunks.content:
There would be 3 exams and an optional final exam. Exam 4 (which is the optional final exam) is provided as an opportunity for improving your grades.
* Exam 1 - 25%
* Exam 2 - 25%
* Exam 3 - 25%
* In-Class Quizzes – 5%
* Homework - 20%
* Exam 4 (Optional Final Exam) - replaces the lowest grade among the 3 exams.

It is important that all the graded tests / assignments are completed on your own, in accordance with the Aggie Honor Code and the rules specified for each assignment. You may not discuss the problems with anyone else until after everyone has finished taking the test/assignment.

chunks.title: Exam Policy
chunks.content:
* All exams and in-class quizzes must be taken in-person in class.
* In-class exams would be closed book and closed notes. You are allowed a one-page single-side handwritten cheatsheet (paper size limited to Letter) for exams.
* In-class quizzes would be closed book and closed notes unless otherwise specified. Cheatsheets are NOT allowed for in-class quizzes.
* Cash-flow tables needed for the exam would be provided along with the test paper and must be returned along with the test paper at the end of the test.
* Hand-held calculators are allowed to be used during exams/quizzes. No other electronic devices can be used during exams/quizzes.
* Mobile phones, tablets, laptops and other interactive electronic devices may NOT be used to serve as a calculator unless an exception has been granted by the instructor for certain quizzes.
* If you do not have a calculator or run into an issue, you may be able to borrow it from Evans Library prior to the test. Plan in advance since availability may fluctuate based on demand. It might also be easier to borrow one from a friend.

chunks.title: Regarding Optional Final Exam
chunks.content:
* There is no penalty for taking the optional final exam.
* The grade for the optional final exam would be used to replace the lowest grade among the first 3 exams only if it would improve your existing grade. If you do not do well on the optional exam, the original grades for the first 3 exams would be retained.
* You are allowed to take the optional final ONLY if you have taken all 3 exams. i.e. Exam 1, Exam 2 and Exam 3.
* If you have missed either Exam 1, Exam 2 or Exam 3 due to a valid university-excused absence (must provide verifiable documentation), then the optional final exam grade would be used to replace the grade for the missing exam. In this case, the optional final exam grade would not be used to replace any other test grade.

chunks.title: Grading Policy for Homework, Quizzes and Exams
chunks.content:
* You must explain the logic and show the steps involved in calculation to demonstrate how you arrived at the answer for every numerical problem on any given assignment. i.e. the steps (including the formula used) must be written in the test paper that you submit for in-class exams/quizzes and in attachment you upload for homework. This also applies to multiple choice questions that have a numerical answer.
* If the work submitted does not clearly indicate your reasoning or outline steps as to how you arrived at the solution, the points for that question would be reduced to zero. This is done in order to discourage guess work and can also help with partial credit.
* For each assignment problem, you need to indicate the corresponding question number for each answer on your written submission.
* Homework assignments are to be submitted electronically through Canvas. In case you face an issue accessing Canvas while within the submission deadline, you can e-mail your homework to the instructor/TA within the deadline.
* Policy regarding late submissions have been outlined below.

chunks.title: Other Information
chunks.content: The grading scheme may be modified at the discretion of the instructor if a situation arises where it may be necessary and any such changes would be announced at least a week in advance.

chunks.title: Late Work Policy
chunks.content: Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy (Student Rule 7).

chunks.title: Course Specific Late Work Policy
chunks.content:
* If you miss any graded quiz/test/assignment, you must contact the instructor as soon as possible with a written authorized excuse. These have to be discussed with me via e-mail and the student must be able to provide all supporting documentation that can be verified. Make up assignments will be given in accordance with University Rules (see Student Rule 7 at http://student-rules.tamu.edu).
* If you have missed either Exam 1, Exam 2 or Exam 3 due to a university-excused absence (must provide verifiable documentation), then the optional final exam grade would be used to replace the grade for the missing exam. In this case, the optional final exam grade would not be used to replace any other test grade.
* The following instructions apply ONLY to Homework Assignments (take-home submissions which constitute 20% of the final grade):
  * You can use up to 1 late day per homework with no penalty, limited to a maximum of 3 late days during the entire semester. Using 1 late day would allow you to submit your homework within 24 hours of the original deadline. You cannot use more than 1 late day on any individual homework assignment. You won't get more than 3 late days in any given semester, so use them wisely.
  * Assignment submissions beyond the given deadline are not accepted unless you are within your limit for late days as outlined below. Hence, plan on submitting all assignments early in case you happen to encounter some kind of technical difficulties later on. Remember that it is your responsibility to ensure that your work has been submitted properly.
  * If you use more than 3 late days during the semester, then that particular homework assignment and any subsequent late homework submissions would not be given any points.
  * Late days DO NOT APPLY to quizzes or exams.
  * In some cases, certain late assignment submissions may be accepted at the discretion of the instructor. Any intention of using more than 3 late days for homework in a given semester would require you to produce verifiable valid documentation for university allowed excuses, including documentation for the prior 3 days as well – with penalty adjudged on a case-by-case basis.

chunks.title: Texas A&M College Station (Mental Health Support)
chunks.content: Students needing a listening ear can contact University Health Services at 979.458.4584. Call 911 or visit your nearest emergency room if you are currently experiencing a life-threatening situation or if your safety is at risk. 24-hour emergency help is also available through the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline (988) or at 988lifeline.org.
